description: New road construction, bypass, junction improvement, or carriageway widening. Appraised using DfT Transport Analysis Guidance (TAG).

junction_improvement.note: Grade-separated junctions at the upper end

bridge_structure.note: Wide range depending on span and complexity

typical_cost.land_acquisition: Use DLUHC land value estimates by region and land type (agricultural, residential, commercial)

typical_benefits.description: Reduced travel time for existing and new traffic
typical_benefits.monetisation: DfT TAG values of time by purpose (work: ~GBP 20/hr, commuting: ~GBP 7/hr, leisure: ~GBP 6/hr). Apply ramp-up over 2-5 years.

typical_benefits.description: Reduced fuel, maintenance from improved road conditions
Fewer accidents from improved geometry, grade separation
typical_benefits.monetisation: DfT TAG accident costs (fatal: ~GBP 2.3m, serious: ~GBP 260k, slight: ~GBP 20k)
Present separately from core BCR (Green Book 2026 requirement)

typical_benefits.description: Net impact on emissions (may be positive or negative)
typical_benefits.monetisation: DESNZ carbon values (pre-discounted, do not double-discount)

default_parameters.additionality_note: Transport schemes use TAG/WebTAG methodology, not HMT additionality adjustments. Induced demand is addressed separately.
default_parameters.benefit_ramp_up_note: TAG assumes traffic builds to forecast levels over 2-5 years post-opening

reference_bcr_range.note: DfT VfM categories: Poor (<1.0), Low (1.0-1.5), Medium (1.5-2.0), High (2.0-4.0), Very High (>4.0). Most approved schemes are Medium or above.
reference_cases: A14 Cambridge to Huntingdon FBC (2014): initial BCR 1.8, adjusted BCR 2.5 with WEIs
A303 Stonehenge Tunnel OBC (2017): BCR 0.45 (proceeded on non-monetised heritage/connectivity grounds)
Lower Thames Crossing FBC (2022): BCR 1.22 (including WEIs)
Typical small junction improvement: BCR 3-6 (low cost, concentrated time savings)

typical_risks: Cost overruns: Flyvbjerg (2003) found average 28% overrun for road projects
Demand forecasting error: Flyvbjerg (2005) found benefits overestimated by 50% on average for transport
Environmental objections (SSSI, AONB, protected species) delaying planning
Ground conditions (especially in river valleys, flood plains)
Induced demand partially offsetting journey time savings
Carbon impact may be net negative (more driving) unless offset by congestion relief

publications: IPA Annual Report on Major Projects
Green Book Supplementary Guidance: Optimism Bias
reference_evaluations: What Works Centre for Local Economic Growth: Transport Evidence Review (2015)
DfT Post Opening Project Evaluation (POPE) reports
